title: Salbutamol (Ventolin)
content_markdown:
##### Type

Synthetic \u03b22 receptor stimulant [S4]

##### Presentations

Pressurised metered dose inhaler (pMDI) – 100mcg per activation

##### Actions

- Bronchodilation

- Relaxation of involuntary muscle

- Moves K+ from extracellular to intracellular space

#### Uses

- Respiratory distress due to asthma or COPD

- Emergency treatment of suspected hyperkalaemia 

#### Adverse Effects

- Tachycardia

- Tremors

- Hypotension

#### Contraindications

Known hypersensitivity 

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

#### Mild to moderate respiratory distress

**pMDI via spacer**

##### Adult ≥6 years

- 4-12 actuations 
- Repeat every 20-30 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.

##### Paediatric <6years

- 2-6 actuations 
- Repeat every 20-30 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.

#### Severe respiratory distress

**pMDI (via spacer or with pMDI adaptor on BVM/CPAP) **

##### Adult ≥6 years

- 12 actuations 
- Repeat every 20 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.

##### Paediatric <6years

- 6 actuations 
- Repeat every 20 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.

#### Life threatening respiratory distress and hyperkalaemia

**pMDI (with pMDT adaptor on BVM/CPAP)**

##### Adult ≥6 years

- 12 actuations 
- Repeat every 10 minutes
- No repeat limit.

##### Paediatric <6 years

- 6 actuations 
- Repeat every 10 minutes
- No repeat limit.

#### Special Notes

- The solution in the pMDI is separated. Please shake prior to every actuation.\u2003
